methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Mara is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Daniel Kofman. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, supplemented by a broader universe of 10 private and 2 public defense-tech firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Saronic.

The peer-median approach, the sole triangulation method applied here, anchors on the median valuation of $225M across the 4-company sample. The sample spans from Tenna Systems at seed stage ($60M) to three Series A firms—Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M)—establishing a $60M to $500M range. This methodology yields a point estimate of $225M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $191M (low), $225M (median), and $259M (high). The single-method basis limits conviction; a broader triangulation incorporating revenue multiples, market TAM analysis, or venture-stage benchmarks would strengthen this view. Key uncertainties include Mara's revenue stage and traction relative to peers, the representativeness of a 4-firm sample in an evolving frontier-tech subsector, and the stage-gap between the seed-stage target and three Series A comparables. The $225M median should be treated as indicative pending disclosure of Mara's financial performance and more granular peer staging.
